mnemonics.title: SQL Clause Order
mnemonics.content: From Where Group Having Select Order (F-W-G-H-S-O) - Remember: 'Frank Went Grocery Hunting, Soon Everyone Ran' (From Where Group Having Select Order)

acronyms.usage: Standard language for managing relational databases
Clauses that groups rows for aggregate operations
Filter condition for grouped results (HAVING = filter after grouping)

visualTips.visualization: Imagine sorting papers into folders - each folder is a group, and you can count/average items in each folder.
WHERE is like screening individual items before grouping; HAVING is like checking each group after items are organized.
Think of them as 'summary calculators' - they take many rows and produce one summary number.

analogies.analogy: Like organizing students by class - you group them first, then can find average height per class.
Like a principal filtering which classes have more than 30 students after classes are formed.
Like a camera with wide-angle lens - it captures the whole scene (all rows) and produces one picture (summary).
